manifesto.line2: We study ingredients, temperature and time.
manifesto.line3: Every dessert is a successful experiment.

about.title: About us
about.subtitle: The Sweet Lab story
about.heroText: A pastry laboratory where French precision meets Milanese soul — and every gram tells a story.

lines: We do not chase trends. We chase balance — the moment when texture, temperature and memory align on the palate.
Every creation begins with a question: what should this taste like at 8 a.m., at room temperature, after the third bite?
Transparency is our luxury. You watch us work because craft deserves to be seen, not hidden behind closed doors.
Sweet Lab is not a shop. It is a salon, a laboratory, and a promise that time spent here will be remembered.

origin.title: Where it began
origin.subtitle: From Parisian kitchens to Via della Spiga
paragraphs: Elena Marchetti spent seven years in Paris — in brigades where butter temperature was measured to the half-degree and where failure was never an option, only a lesson. She returned to Milan with a conviction: the city deserved a patisserie that treated dessert with the same rigour as haute cuisine.

In 2018 she found a narrow storefront on Via della Spiga. The space was modest; the vision was not. Within months, word spread among chefs, collectors and curious neighbours who peered through the glass at 6 a.m. to watch croissants being laminated by hand.

What began as a counter and a small laboratory grew into something rarer: a place where guests could sit, taste, ask questions, and leave understanding why a millefeuille takes three days. The open kitchen was not a marketing choice — it was a statement of honesty.

Today Sweet Lab welcomes visitors from Milan and far beyond. Some come for a single entremets; others return every season for the new menu. All leave with the same impression: that someone, somewhere, cared deeply about their experience.

recipes.label: recipes in our permanent collection, each tested dozens of times before reaching the counter

suppliers.label: partner suppliers across France, Sicily and Piedmont — visited personally every season

tastings.label: guided tastings hosted since we opened the reservation-only salon

awards.label: national and international recognitions for artisan patisserie excellence

about.timelineSubtitle: Five chapters that shaped the maison

2018.title: The doors open
2018.text: After years in Parisian laboratories, Elena Marchetti opens Sweet Lab on Via della Spiga. The first menu is deliberately small — twelve creations, each perfected before the next is introduced. Lines form before opening; the neighbourhood discovers that pastry can be an event.

2019.title: The morning ritual
2019.text: Sweet Lab introduces its dawn counter: croissants, pain au chocolat and seasonal viennoiserie baked before 7 a.m. Regulars arrive with empty boxes and leave with still-warm layers. The laboratory rhythm becomes part of Milan's morning landscape.

2020.title: Laboratory visible
2020.text: The open kitchen becomes the heart of the experience. Reservation-only tastings let guests sit metres from the pass, ask questions, and taste components before they become a whole. Transparency transforms curiosity into loyalty.

2022.title: Recognition
2022.text: National press and industry peers name Sweet Lab among Milan's finest artisan patisseries. The accolade changes nothing in the recipes — only confirms that patience, ingredient quality and visible craft still matter in a world of shortcuts.

2024.title: Seasons & collaborations
2024.text: The seasonal line expands with limited collections tied to harvest calendars — Bronte pistachio in spring, Piedmont hazelnuts in autumn. Collaborations with master chocolatiers bring exclusive bean-to-bar expressions to the salon for the first time.

philosophy.title: What we believe
philosophy.subtitle: Five principles that guide every recipe, every service, every morning in the laboratory.

01.title: Time is an ingredient
01.text: Resting dough, curing ganache, ageing praliné — we refuse to compress timelines that exist for a reason. A tart that needs forty-eight hours gets forty-eight hours.

02.title: Source with intention
02.text: We know our butter producer by name. We visit pistachio orchards in Sicily. Chocolate is selected cup by cup, not by brand logo.

03.title: Teach through taste
03.text: Every tasting is a conversation. We explain lamination, emulsion, tempering — not to impress, but so you leave with a deeper appetite for quality.

04.title: Seasonality as respect
04.text: We do not serve strawberries in January. Our menu follows nature's calendar because flavour peaks when ingredients are at their moment.

05.title: Beauty must be edible
05.text: Visual harmony matters — but never at the expense of texture or balance. A creation that photographs well but disappoints on the fork has failed its purpose.

process.title: From idea to counter
process.subtitle: How a Sweet Lab creation is born

01.title: Brief & inspiration
01.text: Every new recipe starts with a brief: flavour memory, seasonal ingredient, or a guest request. Elena sketches proportions before a single gram is weighed.

02.title: Laboratory trials
02.text: Three to seven iterations in the test kitchen. Each version is tasted blind, annotated, and adjusted — temperature logs included.

03.title: Brigade training
03.text: When a recipe is approved, the full team replicates it independently. Consistency across shifts is non-negotiable.

04.title: Salon introduction
04.text: New creations debut quietly — often first to tasting guests, whose feedback shapes the final presentation and portion size.

05.title: Permanent or seasonal
05.text: Only a fraction join the permanent collection. Others live for a season, then retire — remembered, not repeated.

butter.title: AOP Charentes-Poitou butter
butter.text: 82% fat, cultured, with the nutty depth that makes laminated dough sing. Delivered twice weekly from Normandy.

vanilla.title: Bourbon vanilla from Madagascar
vanilla.text: Plump, oily pods scraped by hand. We use both seeds and spent pods — infused into cream for forty-eight hours.

pistachio.title: Bronte pistachio DOP
pistachio.text: Harvested on volcanic slopes in Sicily. Intense green, almost piney — the backbone of our spring collection.

chocolate.title: Bean-to-bar selection
chocolate.text: Single-origin couvertures from small producers in Ecuador, Peru and Tanzania — chosen for clarity, not cocoa percentage alone.

ingredients.title: Ingredients
ingredients.text: AOP butter, Bourbon vanilla, Bronte pistachio and selected bean-to-bar chocolate. We publish allergen information for every creation and will always tell you the origin of what you eat — because trust begins at the source.

precision.title: Precision
precision.text: Every recipe is calibrated to the gram, with thermal probes in every refrigerator and a logbook for every batch. Pastry is chemistry; we treat it that way — not coldly, but with the respect that repeatable excellence demands.

tradition.title: Tradition
tradition.text: Classic techniques of great French patisserie — pâte feuilletée, crème pâtissière, ganache montée — reinterpreted with contemporary sensitivity. We honour the masters by learning their methods before we dare to evolve them.

craft.title: Visible craft
craft.text: Our laboratory has no back door for guests. You see the whisk, the marble, the tempered chocolate. We believe that watching something made with care makes it taste better — and we have eight years of smiling faces to prove it.

elena.name: Elena Marchetti
elena.bio: Trained under Parisian maîtres in the 8th and 16th arrondissements, Elena returned to Milan with a scientific notebook and an artisan's heart. She still tests every new recipe personally and greets regulars by name at the counter each morning.

marco.name: Marco Vitale
marco.bio: Marco joined Sweet Lab in 2019 after a decade in Turin's finest chocolate ateliers. He oversees tempering curves, praliné textures and the maison's exclusive bean-to-bar collaborations — work that requires the focus of a watchmaker and the palate of a sommelier.
marco.quote: Chocolate speaks in temperatures. My job is to listen.

sofia.name: Sofia Laurent
sofia.bio: Half Milanese, half Lyonnaise, Sofia bridges the laboratory and the guest experience. She designs tasting flows, coordinates private events and ensures that every visitor — whether first-time or weekly — feels welcomed into a home, not a transaction.
sofia.quote: The salon is where craft becomes memory.

cta.title: Come taste the story
cta.text: Book a guided tasting in our laboratory or visit the counter on Via della Spiga. We save a place for the curious.
